Child Transmission of HIV (PMTCT) intervention supports pregnant women living with HIV with timely treatment, peer support, and community follow-up—turning fear into hope and protecting the next generation. GENDER & HUMAN RIGHTS NEPWHAN’s Gender and Human Rights intervention removes barriers to HIV and TB services by empowering communities, supporting survivors, and strengthening access to justice across Nigeria. COMMUNITY-LED MONITORING Through Community-Led Monitoring (CLM), NEPWHAN places people at the center of health system accountability—collecting real-time feedback, resolving service gaps, and improving quality of care across Nigeria. COMMUNITY SYSTEMS STRENGTHENING NEPWHAN’s Community Systems Strengthening ensures communities have the skills, systems, and partnerships needed to sustain health and human rights gains beyond donor funding. ABOUT US The Network of People Living with HIV and AIDS in Nigeria (NEPWHAN) is the umbrella body representing all people living with HIV (PLHIV) in Nigeria. Established in 1998 and formally registered with the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) in 2004, NEPWHAN serves as the collective voice of PLHIV, advocating for their rights, dignity, and access to quality healthcare and social support. With branch offices in all 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Abuja, NEPWHAN operates through a nationwide structure of networks, support groups, and community-based organizations. These structures ensure that the voices of PLHIV are heard at every level — from local communities to national policy platforms.
